Your Digital Tool Stack
I will be recommending digital tools, apps, and websites to help you
implement the 2 Hour Content Ecosystem we will be going over.
As I said, this isn’t a full blown technical writing course. I want this to be as
practical as possible.
I want you to have a guided way of practicing writing, sucking at it, and
learning from your failures — as that is the only path to real results and
lessons learned.
Some of these links will contain affiliate links. I have tested multiple
variations of scheduling apps, note-taking software, and newsletter / blog
software. I will only promote the ones that I give my full endorsement.
For the sake of accessibility, I have curated as many free options as possible.
Those are the tools that the course will be based around.
1) Notion (free) 3% Next post
Completed
Notion is a non-negotiable software to have if you plan on doing anything
online. It is arguably the most powerful app I’ve come across. You can use it
for note-taking, project management, organization, todo lists, client
management, website or blog creation (with certain plugins), and really
anything you can think of.
In this course we will be using the template, The Daily Writer’s Hub, that
I’ve created for you.
We will use this for idea capture, idea development, writing our drafts, and
keeping track of where our writing is in it’s process.
If you are unfamiliar with Notion, watch this video to understand the basics
of the software.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

You can duplicate the Daily Writer's Hub on Notion here.
2) Beehiiv (free)
For the 2 Hour Content Ecosystem to work, we have to choose a software that
allows us to backlog our newsletters.
I personally do this by posting them to my WordPress blog, but that isn’t
necessary, especially at the start. If you want to build a personal blog one day,
awesome, but I wouldn’t worry about it when you are just starting out.
Why do we need to backlog them?
It is the key to effective monetization and not being spammy on the timeline.
90% of my sales and income stem from plugging my products or services in
my newsletter (which is high value and builds trust / authority), then I plug the
backlogged newsletter under my social media content.
That way, I am not only getting more email subscribers and making an income
— but I am delivering the most value possible and pulling people out of the
surface level short-form content trap.
I’ve tried similar software like Revue and Substack.
Revue’s email deliverability sucks and Substack seems to lack customization.
If you don’t like Beehiiv after trying it, test Substack.
I am tool agnostic. Beehiiv isn’t the end all be all.
It is a free option until you are experienced enough to test a new platform.
Email platforms are great because you can download your CSV of subscribers
and upload them to any other platform whenever you please.
Check out Beehiiv here.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

3) TweetHunter (free + paid)
The foundation of my business, writing, and ideas is Twitter (which we will
discuss later).
TweetHunter is how I:
Generate high-performing ideas
Swipe high-performing writing structures
Schedule my tweets and threads in an optimal way (certain times,
auto retweets, and other cool features)
This is an optional software, but my writing wouldn’t be the same without
it.
If you were to invest in one monthly subscription, it would be this.
Not only that, if you are just starting out, the money being pulled out of
your bank will act as accountability to publish your writing daily.
Check out TweetHunter here.
4) Hemingway (free)
I don’t like writing in Notion.
I like having zero distractions, one tab open, and focusing all of my energy
on writing.
Hemingway is a writing aid, like Google docs or Microsoft Word, but a bit
more focused. Some people recommend it, some hate it.
I just like writing in it as it keeps my drafts separate.
I use the highlights and editing features at my own discretion. Sometimes I
leave sentences long, somethings I listen to Hemingway's advice and edit
down.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --
